presentation_variants.presenting_complaint: Sudden shortness of breath and chest pain
Chest pain that gets worse when I breathe in
I've been struggling to breathe since this morning
Sharp chest pain and feeling short of breath
My leg has been swollen and now I can't catch my breath

history_data.hpi: Patient developed sudden onset breathlessness and right-sided pleuritic chest pain 6 hours ago. Reports a long-haul flight 5 days ago followed by several days of right calf pain and swelling. No fever. No preceding viral illness.
patient_quotes: It hurts to take a deep breath.
I just couldn't get enough air all of a sudden.
My leg has been aching and swollen since the flight.
The pain is on one side of my chest.
I've never felt this breathless before.

social_history.exercise: Reduced recently due to leg pain

abnormal_investigations.ECG: Sinus tachycardia. S1Q3T3 pattern. Right heart strain. T-wave inversions in V1–V4.
abnormal_investigations.Troponin: 0.09 ng/mL (mildly elevated — right ventricular strain; indicates higher-risk PE)
abnormal_investigations.Chest X-Ray: Wedge-shaped opacity right lower lobe (Hampton's hump). Focal oligaemia right lower zone (Westermark's sign). No pneumothorax.
abnormal_investigations.CTPA (CT Pulmonary Angiography): Bilateral pulmonary emboli. Saddle embolus at main pulmonary artery bifurcation. Right heart strain on CT. No evidence of infarction.
abnormal_investigations.D-Dimer: 4.2 mg/L FEU (markedly elevated — strongly supports thromboembolic diagnosis)
abnormal_investigations.FBC (Full Blood Count): WBC 10.8 × 10⁹/L, Hb 13.1 g/dL, Platelets 278 × 10⁹/L — mild leukocytosis
abnormal_investigations.Urea & Electrolytes: Na 137 mmol/L, K 3.9 mmol/L, Creatinine 82 µmol/L — normal
abnormal_investigations.Coagulation Screen (PT/APTT/INR): PT 13s, APTT 28s, INR 1.1 — normal, prior to anticoagulation
abnormal_investigations.ABG (Arterial Blood Gas): pH 7.46, pO2 7.8 kPa (hypoxaemia), pCO2 3.6 kPa (hypocapnia), HCO3 22 mmol/L — type 1 respiratory failure
abnormal_investigations.Bilateral Leg Doppler Ultrasound: Right popliteal vein DVT confirmed. Left leg — no DVT.
abnormal_investigations.Echocardiogram: Right ventricular dilatation and hypokinesis. D-sign (interventricular septal shift). Estimated RVSP 48 mmHg. Normal LV function.
